summary: The suite covers six well-chosen scenarios (main, master, feature branch, PR, semver tag, sanitization) and adds useful workflow-structure checks (actionlint, file existence, YAML shape). Exact tag-count assertions are a strong design choice. However, rigor is the main weakness: there are no negative/error-path tests (invalid SHA, empty branch, ValidateNotNullOrEmpty boundary), no test for tags *without* the v prefix (the regex accepts v? but it's never exercised in isolation), no pre-release semver (v1.2.3-beta.1), no combination of PR + semver tag, and no check that the short-SHA truncation at <7 chars works. Design has a significant structural problem: all six functional test cases are funneled through a single monolithic act run and a single BeforeAll block, so one Docker/act failure silently voids every case simultaneously with no isolation. The custom output-format parser (=== TEST CASE N: / TAG:) is complex, untested itself, and tightly coupled to the workflow's echo format — a whitespace change in the workflow would silently produce empty tag lists and all count assertions would fail with cryptic 'No tags parsed' messages. There are also no fast unit tests for the PowerShell logic itself (ConvertTo-DockerTagSafe, semver extraction), meaning the entire suite requires Docker to run at all.
